question_text: Two sound waves are compared: Wave X has a frequency of 256 Hz and Wave Y has a frequency of 512 Hz. Which wave has a higher pitch, and why?
correct_answer: Wave Y has a higher pitch because it has a higher frequency. Pitch increases with frequency.
explanation: Pitch is directly related to frequency—higher frequency produces a higher pitch sound. Since Wave Y (512 Hz) has double the frequency of Wave X (256 Hz), it will sound much higher in pitch.
